title: Senior Business Analyst (m/f/d) Process & Reporting focus
required_skills: Business Analyst experience in transformation projects
Translate business needs into structured requirements
Understanding of data models and reporting architectures
matched_skills: Business Analyst / PMO experience in transformation projects (partial)
Data quality QA and sprint-based delivery (UAT-adjacent)
missing_skills: Functional requirements documentation for ERP/HCM systems
Data model and reporting architecture design
core_responsibilities: Support design of global HC processes (L3/L4) aligned with Workday; document gaps and regional exceptions using SIPOC and BPMN
Translate business needs into functional and reporting requirements; ensure alignment between process design, data structures, and reporting needs
Derive reporting requirements from process design; align with HC stakeholders and Data Warehouse/reporting teams on KPIs, reporting logic, and data models
Support validation of Workday implementation and reporting outputs; participate in UAT and feedback loops with business stakeholders
Act as interface between HC business teams, Process & Data workstream, and IT/Reporting teams; facilitate workshops and requirement discussions
recommended_emphasis: Highlight data quality QA work at Hapag-Lloyd as evidence of structured requirements validation and UAT-adjacent experience
Frame HSBC PMO role around cross-functional requirements gathering and reporting across 6 workstreams
Emphasize experience working in global/cross-regional environments (EMEA, APAC at Minth Group)
Position KPI framework design and executive dashboards as direct relevance to reporting requirements engineering
Acknowledge Workday gap explicitly if cover letter is written; instead lean on transferable HCM-adjacent analytics experience

company_info.size_raw: End client described as 'famous company in the semiconductor sector' — no headcount figure in JD; match_score 45 < 50, LinkedIn lookup skipped
